evals.prompt: Create a PR on GitHub for my current branch and assign it to me after creation. Do not ask me to copy anything into chat.
evals.expected_output: The workflow creates the PR directly on GitHub and assigns the authenticated user as an assignee, not as a reviewer.
expectations: The workflow uses assignee assignment semantics for the authenticated user.
The workflow does not add the authenticated user as a reviewer by default.
The workflow says to update the created PR after creation if the create call cannot set assignees directly.

evals.prompt: Open a pull request for this branch, keep the PR text in English, and make sure I am the assignee on the PR.
evals.expected_output: The workflow keeps PR content in English and uses assignee assignment semantics for the authenticated user.
expectations: The workflow keeps title and description in English.
The workflow assigns the current authenticated user as assignee.
The workflow does not treat reviewer assignment as equivalent to assignee assignment.

evals.prompt: Create the PR directly on GitHub. After it exists, put me on it as the assignee. Do not add me as a reviewer unless I explicitly ask for reviewers.
evals.expected_output: The workflow separates assignee handling from reviewer handling and avoids adding the current user as a reviewer by default.
expectations: The workflow explicitly avoids adding the current authenticated user as reviewer by default.
The workflow uses a follow-up update step to assign the PR when needed.
The workflow allows reviewer assignment only when the user explicitly requests it.

evals.prompt: Create a pull request on GitHub for this branch. I did not say anything about review readiness.
evals.expected_output: The workflow creates the PR as a draft by default when the user did not explicitly request a ready-for-review PR.
expectations: The workflow creates the PR as draft by default when review readiness was not specified.
The workflow prefers an explicit draft flag or draft-capable creation path when one is available.
The workflow reports whether the PR was created as draft or ready for review.

evals.prompt: Create a ready-for-review PR on GitHub for this branch. Do not leave it in draft state.
evals.expected_output: The workflow respects an explicit request for a non-draft PR and does not force the draft default in that case.
expectations: The workflow does not force draft state when the user explicitly requested a ready-for-review PR.
The workflow treats the draft state as a default that can be overridden by explicit user intent.
The workflow reports whether the PR was created as draft or ready for review.
